Implement the following plan:

# Plan: Shared Nav/Footer Build System for justNICE.us

## Context

justNICE.us has 78 static HTML pages with nav and footer copy-pasted inline. Every time a nav link is added (like ICE Tracker), all 73+ files must be edited individually. The nav/footer markup is inconsistent across pages (different active-link styles, missing `data-i18n` attributes, different footer link sets, varying contact form dropdowns).

**Goal**: Extract nav and footer into shared partials with a zero-dependency Node.js build script that injects them at deploy time. Zero client-side JS overhead — pure static HTML output.

## Architecture

### How it works
1. `_partials/` directory holds 4 shared HTML files (nav, footer-full, footer-slim, scripts)
2. Each source page has `<!-- build:config -->`, `<!-- build:nav -->`, `<!-- build:footer -->`, `<!-- build:scripts -->` markers
3. `build.js` (root, ~70 lines, zero deps) reads partials, finds all HTML files, replaces markers, writes to `dist/`
4. GitHub Actions runs `node build.js` before SCP deploy
5. Pages without markers are copied as-is (safe incremental migration)

### Per-page configuration

Each page declares its config in an HTML comment after `<body>`:
```html
<body class="font-sans text-gray-900 bg-white">
<!-- build:config {"nav": "blog", "footer": "full"} -->

<!-- build:nav -->

  <!-- breadcrumbs and page content stay here -->

<!-- build:footer -->
<!-- build:scripts -->
</body>
```

**`nav` values**: `"home"`, `"resources"`, `"kyr"`, `"tracker"`, `"blog"`, `"downloads"` — controls which nav item gets the active underline style
**`footer` values**: `"full"` (donate CTA + contact form + about + copyright) or `"slim"` (about + copyright only)

### Active link handling

The nav partial uses `{{ACTIVE:key}}` tokens in each link's class attribute. The build script replaces the matching token with `underline underline-offset-4 decoration-2` and strips all non-matching tokens. This standardizes the active style across all pages.

### Directory structure
```
justNICE.us/
  _partials/
    nav.html              # Full <header> with desktop + mobile nav
    footer-full.html      # Donate CTA + contact form + about + nav links + copyright
    footer-slim.html      # About + nav links + copyright (no form)
    scripts.html          # Mobile menu toggle + contact form JS + translations init
  build.js                # Node.js build script (zero deps)
  dist/                   # Build output (deployed, .gitignored)
  .github/workflows/deploy.yml  # Updated with build step
```

## Implementation Steps

### Phase 1: Create build infrastructure
1. Create `_partials/nav.html` — extract canonical nav from `index.html`, standardize:
   - All 7 nav items with `data-i18n` attributes
   - `{{ACTIVE:key}}` tokens on each link
   - `h-12` logo (standardized)
   - `/#contact-form` for contact link (works everywhere)
   - Both desktop and mobile navs in one file
2. Create `_partials/footer-full.html` — extract from `index.html`, standardize:
   - Donate CTA section
   - Contact form with all categories (ICE Activity Report, General, Immigrant Resources, KYR, Blog, Other)
   - About section with standardized footer nav links (Resources, KYR, ICE Tracker, Blog, Downloads, NICE Gear, Contact, Donate)
   - Copyright bar with justnice.us, email, GitHub, GD org credit
   - All elements get `data-i18n` attributes
3. Create `_partials/footer-slim.html` — footer without contact form or donate CTA
4. Create `_partials/scripts.html` — mobile menu toggle, contact form handler, translations init
5. Create `build.js` at project root
6. Add `dist/` to `.gitignore`

### Phase 2: Update GitHub Actions
- Add Node.js setup step (`actions/setup-node@v4`, node 20)
- Add `node build.js` step
- Add asset copy step (copy non-HTML files to `dist/`)
- Change SCP source to `dist/*` with `strip_components: 1`

### Phase 3: Migrate top-level pages (5 pages)
For each of: `index.html`, `blog.html`, `downloads.html`, `immigrant-resources.html`, `kyr-ice-training.html`:
1. Add `<!-- build:config {"nav": "...", "footer": "..."} -->` after `<body>`
2. Replace `<header>...</header>` (including mobile menu div) with `<!-- build:nav -->`
3. Replace `<footer>...</footer>` and trailing scripts with `<!-- build:footer -->` + `<!-- build:scripts -->`
4. Keep breadcrumbs and page content intact
5. Remove duplicated inline scripts (menu toggle, form handler, translation init)

Config per page:
- `index.html`: `{"nav": "home", "footer": "full"}`
- `blog.html`: `{"nav": "blog", "footer": "full"}`
- `downloads.html`: `{"nav": "downloads", "footer": "slim"}`
- `immigrant-resources.html`: `{"nav": "resources", "footer": "full"}`
- `kyr-ice-training.html`: `{"nav": "kyr", "footer": "full"}`

### Phase 4: Migrate ICE tracker pages (56 pages)
Write a one-time migration script that:
1. Finds all `blog/ice-activity/*.html` and `blog/ice-activity/texas/*.html`
2. Strips `<header>...</header>` block and replaces with `<!-- build:nav -->`
3. Strips `<footer>...</footer>` and trailing scripts, replaces with `<!-- build:footer -->` + `<!-- build:scripts -->`
4. Inserts `<!-- build:config {"nav": "tracker", "footer": "full"} -->` after `<body>`

All get `{"nav": "tracker", "footer": "full"}`.

### Phase 5: Migrate blog posts (~15 pages)
Same migration approach. All get `{"nav": "blog", "footer": "full"}`.

### Phase 6: Verify and deploy
1. Run `node build.js` locally
2. Verify `dist/` output: spot-check 5 pages (index, a state page, a blog post, downloads, kyr)
3. Check all pages have correct nav, footer, active links, `data-i18n` attributes
4. Commit and push — GitHub Actions builds and deploys
5. Purge Cloudflare cache
6. Verify live pages

## Key files to modify
- `index.html` — reference for extracting canonical nav/footer partials; then strip inline nav/footer
- `downloads.html` — reference for slim footer; then strip
- `blog/ice-activity/texas.html` — representative state page for bulk migration script
- `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` — add build step
- `blog/*.html` (15 files) — strip inline nav/footer
- `blog/ice-activity/*.html` + `blog/ice-activity/texas/*.html` (56 files) — strip inline nav/footer

## New files to create
- `_partials/nav.html`
- `_partials/footer-full.html`
- `_partials/footer-slim.html`
- `_partials/scripts.html`
- `build.js`
- `.gitignore` (add `dist/`)

## New page template

Future pages only need `<head>` + page content:
```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en" class="scroll-smooth">
<head>
  <!-- meta, title, Tailwind CDN, fonts -->
</head>
<body class="font-sans text-gray-900 bg-white">
<!-- build:config {"nav": "blog", "footer": "full"} -->
<!-- build:nav -->

  <main>
    <!-- your content -->
  </main>

<!-- build:footer -->
<!-- build:scripts -->
</body>
</html>
```

## Verification
- `node build.js` produces `dist/` with all 78 pages
- Every page in `dist/` has the canonical nav and footer HTML (no `{{ACTIVE:...}}` tokens remaining)
- Active links are correctly highlighted per page
- `data-i18n` attributes present on all nav/footer elements
- Contact form submits to `https://n8n.cloudpublica.org/webhook/justnice-contact`
- Mobile menu toggle works
- Language switcher works
- No broken links in nav or footer
- GitHub Actions deploys successfully
- Live site renders correctly on desktop and mobile


If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/kristinesocall/.claude/projects/-Users-kristinesocall-Library-CloudStorage-ProtonDrive-gifteddreamers-proton-me-folder-2025-12-18-CC-kristinesocall/cc38197f-7723-433e-aac6-d50b4464a5a8.jsonl

file.content:
name: Deploy to AWS Lightsail

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: deploy-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Deploy static files
        uses: appleboy/scp-action@v0.1.7
        with:
          host: ${{ secrets.AWS_HOST }}
          username: ${{ secrets.AWS_USER }}
          key: ${{ secrets.AWS_SSH_KEY }}
          source: "index.html,immigrant-resources.html,kyr-ice-training.html,blog.html,blog/*,downloads.html,downloads/*,resources.json,translations.js,assets/*,robots.txt,sitemap.xml,nginx.conf,env.example.js,CONTRIBUTING.md"
          target: "/home/admin/commoncloud/sites/justnice.us"

ICE Activity in Texas

Texas has become the epicenter of federal immigration enforcement under the Trump administration’s second term. With SB 8 mandating sheriff participation in 287(g), daily ICE arrests jumping from 85 to 176, and mega-detention facilities under construction, the state is experiencing enforcement at a scale not seen in modern history. This hub tracks statewide developments and links to detailed regional pages.

<!-- Scripts — shared partial -->

  <!-- Mobile Menu Toggle -->
  <script>
    (function () {
      var menuBtn = document.getElementById('mobile-menu-btn');
      var mobileMenu = document.getElementById('mobile-menu');
      var hamburgerIcon = document.getElementById('hamburger-icon');
      var closeIcon = document.getElementById('close-icon');
      if (!menuBtn || !mobileMenu) return;

      menuBtn.addEventListener('click', function () {
        var isOpen = !mobileMenu.classList.contains('hidden');
        mobileMenu.classList.toggle('hidden');
        if (hamburgerIcon) hamburgerIcon.classList.toggle('hidden');
        if (closeIcon) closeIcon.classList.toggle('hidden');
        menuBtn.setAttribute('aria-expanded', !isOpen);
      });

      // Close mobile menu when clicking an anchor link
      mobileMenu.querySelectorAll('a[href^="#"], a[href*="#"]').forEach(function (link) {
        link.addEventListener('click', function () {
          mobileMenu.classList.add('hidden');
          if (hamburgerIcon) hamburgerIcon.classList.remove('hidden');
          if (closeIcon) closeIcon.classList.add('hidden');
          menuBtn.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');
        });
      });
    })();
  </script>

  <!-- Anonymous Contact Form Submission -->
  <script>
    function submitContactForm(event) {
      event.preventDefault();
      var messageEl = document.getElementById('contact-message');
      var nameEl = document.getElementById('contact-name');
      var emailEl = document.getElementById('contact-email');
      var urlEl = document.getElementById('contact-url');
      var categoryEl = document.getElementById('contact-category');
      var statusEl = document.getElementById('form-status');
      var message = messageEl.value.trim();

      if (!message) return;

      statusEl.textContent = 'Sending...';
      statusEl.className = 'mt-3 text-sm text-gray-400';

      fetch('https://n8n.cloudpublica.org/webhook/justnice-contact', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify({
          message: message,
          name: nameEl ? nameEl.value.trim() : '',
          email: emailEl ? emailEl.value.trim() : '',
          url: urlEl ? urlEl.value.trim() : '',
          category: categoryEl ? categoryEl.value : '',
          source: 'justnice.us',
          page: window.location.pathname,
          timestamp: new Date().toISOString()
        })
      })
      .then(function (response) {
        if (response.ok) {
          statusEl.textContent = 'Thank you! Your message has been sent.';
          statusEl.className = 'mt-3 text-sm text-green-400';
          messageEl.value = '';
          if (nameEl) nameEl.value = '';
          if (emailEl) emailEl.value = '';
          if (urlEl) urlEl.value = '';
          if (categoryEl) categoryEl.value = '';
        } else {
          throw new Error('Network response was not ok');
        }
      })
      .catch(function () {
        statusEl.textContent = 'Something went wrong. Please try again.';
        statusEl.className = 'mt-3 text-sm text-red-400';
      });
    }
  </script>

  <script src="/translations.js"></script>
  <script>if (typeof initTranslation === 'function') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', initTranslation);</script>
